result.summary: Snowflake exposes account-scoped HTTP APIs (general Snowflake REST APIs, SQL API, and Cortex REST API), an open-source Snowflake CLI, and a Snowflake-managed MCP server; documented auth includes Snowflake key-pair JWTs, OAuth access tokens, and programmatic access tokens, with CLI/MCP mechanics only partially exposed in the scraped docs.
result.description: Snowflake is a cloud data platform for storing, querying, and governing data, with additional developer, AI, and application-building capabilities. It also provides AI services such as Cortex for inference, agents, and related tooling within Snowflake accounts.

snowflake_pat.label: Snowflake programmatic access token
snowflake_pat.setup: Create a programmatic access token in Snowflake following [Using programmatic access tokens for authentication](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/programmatic-access-tokens). This token can be used as a bearer-style API token for Snowflake endpoints that support PATs, including Snowflake REST APIs and the SQL API.

snowflake_keypair.label: Snowflake key-pair JWT
snowflake_keypair.setup: Generate an RSA key pair and assign the public key to your Snowflake user as described in [Key-pair authentication and key-pair rotation](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/key-pair-auth). Then create and sign a JWT with that private key for API calls that accept Snowflake JWT authentication.

snowflake_oauth.label: Snowflake OAuth access token
snowflake_oauth.setup: Register or configure a Snowflake OAuth client and obtain an access token as described in [Configure Snowflake OAuth for custom clients](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/oauth-custom) and the broader [OAuth](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/oauth-intro) docs. Use the resulting access token in API requests to Snowflake surfaces that accept OAuth.

surfaces.name: Snowflake SQL API
surfaces.notes: The SQL API is a separate REST API for executing SQL statements. The endpoint is account-scoped per the SQL API endpoint docs, but the scraped excerpt did not include the exact templated base URL.

surfaces.name: Snowflake Cortex REST API
surfaces.notes: Docs explicitly list endpoints under this base path, including `/chat/completions`, `/messages`, and the vector embedding endpoint. Authentication reuses Snowflake REST API auth methods.

surfaces.name: Snowflake-managed MCP server
surfaces.notes: Snowflake documents a managed MCP server product and states it supports MCP revision `2025-11-25`, but the scraped page did not reveal the concrete client connect endpoint URL or explicit authentication mechanics.

surfaces.name: Snowflake CLI

packages.runtimeHint: Python-based CLI; install methods are documented by Snowflake

surfaces.notes: Snowflake CLI is an open-source CLI for developer workflows against Snowflake. The scraped pages confirm installation and connection docs exist, but the truncated excerpts did not expose the exact supported login/authentication commands or environment variable bindings.

surfaces.name: Snowflake REST APIs
surfaces.notes: Snowflake documents this as a suite of REST APIs across many resources. Docs indicate authentication uses an `Authorization` header containing a token such as a JWT, OAuth token, or programmatic access token. The docs scraped did not expose a single canonical shared base URL for the whole suite; Snowflake APIs are account-scoped.
